summary:
The correct answer is \(\textbf{dideoxygenucleotides}\). These do not participate directly in the process of PCR but instead play an integral role elsewhere such as termination synthesis in genetic sequencing techniques like those using Sanger method or next-generation sequencing technologies designed specifically with ddNTP incorporation capabilities enabled via specialized enzymes tailored towards this function alone within their respective methodologies aimed at generating accurate base pair sequences from genomic material under investigation without interfering with standard PCR processes which rely solely upon conventional NTP substrates including ATP/GMP/CTP/Uridine triphosphate among others depending contextually appropriate per specific experimental design requirements specified prior initiating any molecular biology workflow involving these reagents amongst many other potential variables influencing overall outcome quality achievable therewithin said workflows conducted consistently across multiple replicative cycles necessary achieving desired level precision needed meeting stated objectives outlined initially guiding entire project scope defined ex ante commencing initial stages planning phase preceding actual hands-on laboratory work execution phases thereafter concluding final analysis reporting stage marking end point completion milestone reached signifying successful accomplishment goals set forth earlier establishing clear direction focus throughout duration undertaking research endeavor pursued herein described succinctly above covering core aspects pertinent addressing original query posed regarding identification non-PCR component listed alongside traditional ones commonly recognized universally accepted standards governing practice application thereof widely adopted globally spanning diverse fields ranging biotechnology pharmaceutical sciences agriculture environmental monitoring forensics etc... 

In summary,
The item **not** employed in Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR):
is
\[ 
\mathbf{\text{{dideoxyribonucleotides}}}
\] This substance serves distinct purposes outside typical PCR procedures focusing primarily around terminating nascent strands generated durably enabling precise determination individual bases
